# UC-22.21.10 · Algorithm Change Documentation Linkage (Art. 13)

## Description

Ensures each production model_version has linked change_ticket and approver for algorithm change documentation.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for EU AI Act compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes ai_governance and sourcetypes ai:deploy:gov. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Require CI/CD to emit governance event; (2) validate ticket regex against ITSM; (3) create lookup fallback from DB Connect ITSM export; (4) quarterly audit sample. Indexes required: `index=ai_governance`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=ai:deploy:gov`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk DB Connect (2686).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=ai_governance` HEC — model_version, change_ticket, approver, deployed_at.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Require CI/CD to emit governance event; (2) validate ticket regex against ITSM; (3) create lookup fallback from DB Connect ITSM export; (4) quarterly audit sample.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ai_governance sourcetype="ai:deploy:gov" earliest=-90d
| stats latest(deployed_at) as deployed_at, values(approver) as approvers by model_version, change_ticket
| eval ticket_ok=if(match(change_ticket,"^(CHG|CR)-\d+"),1,0)
| where ticket_ok=0 OR mvcount(approvers)<1
| sort - deployed_at
```

Understanding this SPL

**Algorithm Change Documentation Linkage (Art. 13) (EU AI Act)** — Ensures each production model_version has linked change_ticket and approver for algorithm change documentation.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=ai_governance` HEC — model_version, change_ticket, approver, deployed_at. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk DB Connect (2686). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ai_governance; **sourcetype**: ai:deploy:gov. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=ai_governance, sourcetype="ai:deploy:gov", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by model_version, change_ticket** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **ticket_ok** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where ticket_ok=0 OR mvcount(approvers)<1` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with audit evidence in the source system (DLP, IDP, or application) and, if this use case underpins a control test, the ServiceNow GRC console or the evidence pack for a known time window.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (violations), Bar chart (ticket_ok rate by month), Single value (open violations)

## SPL

```spl
index=ai_governance sourcetype="ai:deploy:gov" earliest=-90d
| stats latest(deployed_at) as deployed_at, values(approver) as approvers by model_version, change_ticket
| eval ticket_ok=if(match(change_ticket,"^(CHG|CR)-\d+"),1,0)
| where ticket_ok=0 OR mvcount(approvers)<1
| sort - deployed_at
```

## Visualization

Table (violations), Bar chart (ticket_ok rate by month), Single value (open violations)
